data.title: Why Is AppLovin Stock (APP) Rising in Pre-Market Today, 2/20/26?
data.description:
AppLovin (APP) shares climbed more than 4% in premarket trading on Friday, extending gains from Thursday. The rise follows reports that the company is developing a new social networking platform. Investor interest was further boosted by rumors that OpenAI mentioned during a capital-raise call that it is partnering with AppLovin to monetize ChatGPT through ads, sparking strong market attention.

data.title: Starbucks Stock (SBUX) Loses Froth as it Ramps Up Store Expansion in Loss-Making India
data.description:
Shares in coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) lost some froth today as it looked to double-down its store openings in India despite increasing losses in the world's most populous nation.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starbucks Local Tastes

According to a report in Bloomberg, Starbucks' India arm – a joint-venture with Tata called Tata Starbucks – is looking to add more stores across India despite the unit struggling to make a profit.

Not only is it adding more stores, said the report, it is also looking to re-jig its menu to appeal more to Indian customers. That means local-style offerings such as protein-foam coffee paired with Mumbai-style buns, chai-offerings and more value-focused drinks.

Tata Starbucks ended 2025 with 504 stores across 81 cities in India making it the biggest operator in the country. It added twelve stores during the third quarter ended 31 December, and achieved 7% year-on-year revenue growth. It has a goal of hitting 1,000 stores by 2028. However, losses nearly doubled to $16.5 million in the year to March.

"India remains a focus area as much if not more," said Tata Starbucks Chief Executive Officer Sushant Dash. He added that it will now prioritize expansion over profitability as investment accelerates.

This shows the potential Starbucks executives see in India as a strategic growth market – and why not? It has nearly 1.5 billion people and a burgeoning coffee market.

According to reports, the India Coffee Market was valued at $478 million in 2022 and is expected to reach $1,227.47 million by 2032.

Domestic Drinking Squeeze

Domestic coffee consumption in India has increased substantially in the last five years owing to the growing middle class and increasing awareness of coffee culture. The branded coffee and specialty café market in India is projected to grow at a double‑digit rate into the next decade.

There are challenges however such as rising competition from local chains and lower-cost formats. India's economic slowdown is another factor with ongoing inflation, flat wages and consumers – especially the urban middle class – feeling the squeeze.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Edge Lower Ahead of Key Inflation Data, Tariff Ruling
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended lower on Friday morning ahead of the key Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index and a possible ruling by the Supreme Court on President Donald Trump's tariffs. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.08%, 0.05%, and 0.07%, respectively, at 8:25 a.m. EST on February 20.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages closed lower amid rising geopolitical tensions. The DJIA and the S&P 500 declined 0.54% and 0.28%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.31%. Stocks snapped a three-day winning streak for both the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones, with oil prices reaching a six-month high amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions.

Turning to economic data, traders await key releases, including the fourth-quarter GDP, projected at 2.5% growth following Q3's stronger-than-expected 4.4% rise. Also, economists forecast a 2.8% annual gain in headline PCE and 3% in core PCE.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Why Is AIOS Tech Stock Up Today?
data.description:
AIOS Tech (AIOS) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday despite a lack of news from the artificial intelligence (AI) and technology-driven professional services company. There are no press releases or filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) that explain why the stock rallied today.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead, it looks like retail traders are behind today's activity. AIOS stock has experienced heavy trading in the pre-market, with more than 22 million shares traded. To put that in perspective, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 24,000 units.

Investors will also note that the interest in AIOS Tech comes after a recent transformation. The company has changed its focus to AI services from financing solutions. This also resulted in a name and ticker change last week. Prior to this, the company was named Nisun International Enterprise Development Group and traded under the NISN ticker.

AIOS Tech Stock Movement Today

AIOS Tech stock was up 49.02% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 3.05% rally yesterday. However, the stock has fallen 57.94% year-to-date and 94.16% over the past 12 months.

The volatility that AIOS Tech stock has experienced is a red flag, as is its shift to AI. It suggests the company is chasing a trend amid failure, rather than making a well-thought-out and strategic shift.
data.author: William White

data.title: Copart Stock (CPRT) Is Crashing on Earnings Miss and Analyst Downgrades
data.description:
Copart (CPRT) stock is crashing after the online vehicle auction company reported weak financial results that led analysts to downgrade the shares.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For its fiscal second quarter, Copart reported earnings per share (EPS) of $0.36, which missed the $0.39 consensus forecast of analysts. The company's profit fell 10% from a year earlier. Revenue for the period totaled $1.12 billion, which was short of the $1.15 billion forecast on Wall Street. Sales declined 3.6% from a year ago.

Copart's latest operating margin of 34.7% was down from 36.6% in the same quarter a year earlier, while its free cash flow margin of 5.2% was unchanged. Despite the dismal showing, Copart maintains a strong balance sheet with cash on hand of $5.10 billion at quarter's end.

Analysts' Reaction

The reaction of analysts to Copart's latest print was swift and harsh. Baird lowered its price target on CPRT stock to $48 from $52, citing the weak quarterly results. However, Baird analyst Craig Kennison maintained a Buy-equivalent outperform rating on the shares, saying that the valuation is getting more attractive.

Barrington analyst Gary Prestopino issued a Hold rating on CPRT stock, noting the company's mixed financial performance. Heading into the latest financial results, Copart's stock had hit a 52-week low of $36.52, with the share price falling a further 10% on Feb. 20 after its disappointing print.

Is CPRT Stock a Buy?

Copart's stock has a consensus Hold rating among four Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy, two Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average CPRT price target of $42 implies 11.55% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock (LLY) Rises as Company Reports Progress with Crohn's Disease Treatment
data.description:
Eli Lilly's (LLY) stock moved slightly higher after the pharmaceutical giant reported making progress with a new treatment for Crohn's Disease.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Data from a trial showed that more than 90% of patients who achieved steroid-free remission at one year maintained steroid-free control through three years when taking Eli Lilly's new Crohn's medication. The data also revealed low surgery and hospitalization rates among patients taking the drug for both Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.

Eli Lilly has touted its Omvoh treatment as having the potential to change the disease trajectory of Crohn's Disease, which is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease characterized by persistent swelling and irritation in the digestive tract.

Eli Lilly's Drug Pipeline

Omvoh is currently the only IL-23p19 inhibitor to show strong efficacy in people afflicted with Crohn's Disease. It will still be some time before the new Crohn's treatment is commercially available. But it further bolsters Eli Lilly's drug pipeline.

While widely known for its weight-loss drug Zepbound, Eli Lilly has a robust pipeline of other medications that are used to treat conditions ranging from cancer to Alzheimer's Disease. Wall Street analysts cite the strong drug pipeline when evaluating LLY stock.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Canada's Aritzia (ATZ) Muscles into America With Purchase of Hollywood Fashion House Fred Segal
data.description:
Canadian clothing retailer Aritzia (TSE:ATZ) has purchased U.S. fashion brand Fred Segal.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Terms of the acquisition weren't disclosed, but the purchase comes as Vancouver-based Aritzia expands into the American market. Fred Segal sells trendy clothes that are popular with Hollywood celebrities. People known to wear Fred Segal include Kendall Jenner, Brad Pitt, and Julia Roberts.

Aritzia said the acquisition gives it control of Fred Segal's flagship store on Melrose Ave. in Los Angeles. The Melrose boutique has been referred to as "iconic" and a "Los Angeles landmark" by people in the fashion world. Fred Segal is named after its founder and has been in business since the 1960s.

Aritzia's U.S. Expansion

Management at Aritzia has made it clear that its goal is to expand from its Canadian base into the U.S. Over the past year, the company has opened about 15 boutique retail locations in America. Management has estimated that its U.S. customer base has increased 40% over the past year.

Aritzia's revenue from America has risen more than 45% since 2024, and the company still has a tiny presence in the U.S. market and lots of room to grow. ATZ stock has been a standout among clothing retailers and is one of the best-performing Canadian stocks. In the last 12 months, Aritzia's share price has risen 90%.

Is ATZ Stock a Buy?

Aritzia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 12 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average ATZ price target of C$151.46 implies 20% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
